cards.back: An iterative and incremental software development methodology that emphasizes flexibility and collaboration
Short iterations in an Agile project, typically lasting 1-4 weeks, used to plan and complete specific tasks
A specific Agile framework used for project management, emphasizing roles, ceremonies, and artifacts
A role in Scrum responsible for prioritizing and refining the product backlog, making decisions about what to include in the next sprint
A brief description of a feature or requirement from the user's perspective, used to guide development decisions in Agile projects
